sections.paragraph:
ἐξαπλόω pf. pass. ptc. ἐξηπλωμένος to lay out in such a way that no part is concealed, unfold, spread out (ἁπλόω ‘make single, spread out’, s. ἁπλοῦς; Ps.-Lucian, Philopatr. 17; Galen, De Temper. 1 p. 552 [p. 27, 12 Helmr.]; Herm. Wr. p. 436, 9 Sc.; Suda) a linen cloth Hv 3, 1, 4.—DELG s.v. ἁπλόος.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 345
